behaviors.description: Image containing explicit content is flagged and held for review, not published
behaviors.justification: Real failure mode: the vision model fails to classify explicit content correctly. Mocking the model would hide this—a mock always returns what you tell it to, but a real model might misclassify or return unexpected formats. Only calling the real vision model on a real explicit image reveals whether classification actually works. Single model call (not multiple steps), so Agentic, not Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real vision model with an image containing explicit content; verify it returns explicit classification and the post is held for review (not published or dropped).

behaviors.description: Text post containing hate speech is flagged regardless of obfuscation techniques (e.g., letter substitution, unicode lookalikes)
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the text model doesn't actually handle obfuscated hate speech—it might miss leetspeak variants or unicode homograph tricks. Mocking would hide this because a mock can be programmed to always return 'hate_speech', but the real model might not. Must call the real text model with actual obfuscated inputs to test robustness. Single model call, so Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Call real text model with multiple obfuscated hate speech samples (leetspeak, unicode lookalikes, etc.); verify all are correctly flagged as hate speech.

behaviors.description: A post that passes both content checks is published rather than held or silently dropped
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the orchestration logic is broken—safe posts are held or silently lost. The failure is in the decision logic and publish flow, not in the models' ability to classify. Mock both models to return 'safe' and test that the downstream orchestration (hold → publish decision → database write) works correctly. Models don't need to be real; the wiring does. This is integration of multiple modules with local storage.
behaviors.test_description: With mocked models returning safe verdicts for both image and text, submit a post and verify it reaches the published state (not held in review queue and not silently dropped).

behaviors.description: The full moderation pipeline — image check, text check, publish decision — completes within 5 seconds of submission
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the pipeline is slow due to network latency, external API delays, database operations, or code inefficiency. Mocking all external calls (Integration) would always be fast and hide real bottlenecks. Must call real external services (models via HTTP, real database via network/subprocess) to measure actual latency. Performance depends on real I/O, subprocess, and external API behavior—System level.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a post through the full pipeline with real model API calls and real database operations; measure end-to-end time from submission to publish decision; verify it completes within 5 seconds.

behaviors.description: When the AI vision model is unavailable, image posts are queued for retry rather than auto-approved
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the system auto-approves or silently drops posts when the model is unavailable, or the retry queue logic is broken. Mock the vision model to raise a timeout or connection exception, verify the post enters a retry queue. Mocking the failure is sufficient to test the error-handling path; we don't need real network failure to verify the retry mechanism works. The failure mode is in the application logic (what to do when model is down), not in the infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the vision model to raise a timeout/unavailable exception; submit an image post and verify it enters the retry queue (not auto-approved, not permanently dropped).

behaviors.description: The moderation decision for a borderline image is consistent when the same image is submitted multiple times within a short window
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the system doesn't maintain consistency—either the caching/deduplication logic is broken, or the real model itself is non-deterministic for borderline cases. Mocking the model to always return the same verdict would hide both failures. Must call the real vision model multiple times on the same borderline image to test true consistency—whether it's due to caching or to the model's actual behavior. Multiple calls to the same real model reveal whether the system maintains consistency despite LLM non-determinism.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a borderline image (soft adult, ambiguous context) to the real vision model multiple times in quick succession; verify the moderation decision is consistent across submissions (same verdict each time).
